rationale: The citing paper uses Voss et al. (2003) in two concrete ways: to support the claim that hedonic and utilitarian value are separable dimensions rather than a single continuum, and to ground measurement decisions. In a pretest, Okada explicitly benchmarks single-item hedonic/utilitarian measures against Voss et al.'s multi-item scales and reports reliability patterns consistent with that prior work before choosing parsimonious single-item measures. This role goes beyond a passing background mention because it informs both conceptual setup and measurement validity in the empirical studies.
relation_description: Okada builds on Voss et al.'s two-dimensional conceptualization of hedonic and utilitarian attitudes and uses their scale as a validation benchmark for her own measures. The cited paper functions as conceptual and measurement groundwork for the citing paper's design.
